data.title: Arthur Hayes: Bitcoin in 'No-Trade Zone' Amid AI Disruption and Iran Conflict
data.description: Arthur Hayes has described Bitcoin as being in a "no-trade zone" at approximately $75,000. According to NS3.AI, Hayes attributes this stance to the ongoing AI disruption and the conflict in Iran, which are contributing to an uncertain market outlook. He further stated that he is refraining from deploying new capital until the Federal Reserve takes action to inject liquidity into the economy.

data.title: CFTC Chair Vows Strict Enforcement Against Fraud in Prediction Markets
data.description: CFTC Chair Michael Selig has assured House lawmakers that the agency will employ "the full force of the law" to combat fraud and insider trading in prediction markets. According to NS3.AI, this statement comes amid increased scrutiny following approximately $500 million in oil and equities futures trades on March 23. Selig highlighted that the CFTC has issued an advance notice of proposed rulemaking to establish clearer standards for event contracts. He also emphasized that the agency retains the authority to reject self-certified listings. Despite being the sole commissioner, Selig affirmed that he will not halt rulemaking activities.

data.title: Fed Rate Decision: High Probability of No Change in April, CME Reports
data.description: The CME's FedWatch tool indicates a 99.5% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current interest rates in April, with only a 0.5% chance of a 25 basis point hike. According to Jin10, the likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut by June stands at 1.4%, while the probability of rates remaining unchanged is 98%. Additionally, there is a 0.5% chance of a cumulative 25 basis point increase by June.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 75,000 USDT with a 0.33% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 16, 2026, 21:50 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 75,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 75,000.007813 USDT, with a narrowed 0.33% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Yuga Labs Appoints New CEO to Drive Metaverse Growth
data.description: Yuga Labs has announced the appointment of Michael Figge as its new chief executive officer, while Greg Solano transitions to the role of chairman after serving as CEO for approximately two years. According to NS3.AI, Figge has been in the CEO position for several weeks and intends to concentrate on expanding Otherside, Yuga Labs' metaverse initiative.

data.title: SpaceX Accelerates Employee Stock Vesting Amid IPO Preparations
data.description: SpaceX has reportedly moved up the vesting date for employee stock options to as early as next week, according to sources familiar with the matter. According to Jin10, this decision comes as the company prepares for an initial public offering (IPO) with a target valuation exceeding $2 trillion. Employees were informed that the vesting date for options, which allows them to sell their shares, has been advanced from the expected May date to April. Sources indicate that this accelerated vesting is expected to alleviate employee concerns regarding the number of shares available for sale at the time of the IPO. Typically, companies disclose the number of shares available for sale and the stock options granted to employees in their IPO filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The sources noted that discussions are still ongoing, and details of the IPO, including the timeline, may still change.

data.title: Kalshi Appeals Nevada's Ban on Event Contracts in Ninth Circuit Court
data.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit recently heard oral arguments regarding Kalshi's appeal against Nevada's prohibition on certain event contracts. According to NS3.AI, Kalshi contended that these contracts should be classified as swaps under the jurisdiction of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), rather than being subject to state gaming laws. The court has yet to issue a decision on the matter.

data.title: HIVE Digital Technologies to Raise $75 Million for Expansion
data.description: HIVE Digital Technologies has announced its intention to raise $75 million through a private placement of exchangeable notes. According to NS3.AI, the company plans to allocate the funds towards purchasing GPUs, developing data centers, and making other capital investments. This move is part of HIVE's strategy to enhance its infrastructure and expand its operations in the digital technology sector.

data.title: Circle's Role in DeFi Exploit Sparks Crypto Regulation Debate
data.description: Emily Mason highlights the ongoing debate surrounding Circle Internet Group Inc.'s actions during a recent DeFi exploit, emphasizing the broader implications for crypto regulation. According to NS3.AI, Jeremy Allaire, CEO of Circle, finds himself at the forefront of a conflict between the ideals of permissionless cryptocurrency and the demands of mainstream policy. This situation underscores the tension between maintaining decentralized principles and adhering to regulatory frameworks, a challenge that continues to shape the future of the crypto industry.

data.title: Citi Analyst Recommends Gold and Bitcoin Allocation for Portfolio Efficiency
data.description: Citi analyst Alex Saunders has suggested that a 5% allocation to gold can enhance portfolio efficiency. According to NS3.AI, Saunders emphasized that dividing this exposure between gold and Bitcoin yields even better outcomes. Citi's analysis indicates that this mixed allocation is beneficial during bond bull markets and bear-steepening cycles, which are associated with fiscal concerns and increasing inflation risks.

data.title: Meta to Hold Online Annual Meeting on May 27, SEC Filing Reveals
data.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is set to conduct its annual meeting online on May 27, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). According to Jin10, the meeting will be held virtually, allowing shareholders to participate remotely. This move aligns with the ongoing trend of companies opting for virtual meetings to ensure broader accessibility and convenience for stakeholders. The SEC filing provides details on the agenda and procedures for the meeting, emphasizing the company's commitment to transparency and shareholder engagement. Participants will have the opportunity to discuss Meta's strategic direction and financial performance, as well as vote on key proposals. The decision to hold the meeting online reflects Meta's adaptation to the evolving digital landscape and its focus on leveraging technology to enhance corporate governance.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Expands Crypto Integration into Daily Operations
data.description: Morgan Stanley is increasingly incorporating cryptocurrency into its daily business activities, according to Amy Oldenburg, the head of digital asset strategy. According to NS3.AI, Oldenburg highlighted that the bank is integrating crypto and tokenization into its existing infrastructure while addressing challenges related to wallet, custody, data, and compliance. Additionally, Morgan Stanley is broadening client access beyond bitcoin exchange-traded products to include a wider range of offerings. Notably, the bank's MSBT bitcoin ETF surpassed $100 million in its first six days.

data.title: Alcoa Reports Lower Than Expected Q1 Revenue
data.description: Alcoa Inc. reported its first-quarter revenue at $3.19 billion, falling short of market expectations of $3.27 billion. According to Jin10, the company's financial performance was below analysts' forecasts, reflecting challenges in the aluminum industry. The discrepancy between actual revenue and projections highlights the volatility and unpredictability in the sector. Alcoa's results may prompt further analysis of market conditions and strategic adjustments moving forward.

data.title: Netflix Maintains Full-Year Revenue Forecast
data.description: Netflix has kept its full-year revenue forecast unchanged at $50.7 billion to $51.7 billion. According to Jin10, the market had anticipated a revenue of $51.37 billion. This decision reflects Netflix's confidence in its financial outlook for the year.

data.title: Polymarket Trader Profits $320,000 from Biden Pardon Bets
data.description: A Polymarket trader has reportedly earned $320,000 by accurately predicting U.S. President Joe Biden's final pardons, according to NS3.AI. The trader utilized two linked wallets, identified by Bubblemaps, to place successful bets on four last-minute pardons. Additionally, the trader had previously wagered that Biden would pardon Hunter Biden. This discovery has intensified scrutiny over the timing of political bets, as CFTC Chair Mike Selig is questioned by Congress about the potential need for investigations into such trades.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Companies Increase BTC Sales Amid Industry Challenges
data.description:
Publicly traded Bitcoin mining companies have sold more Bitcoin (BTC) in the first quarter of 2026 than in all four quarters of 2025, reflecting tightening business conditions within the mining industry. According to Cointelegraph, these companies, including MARA, CleanSpark, Riot, Cango, Core Scientific, and Bitdeer, collectively sold over 32,000 BTC in Q1 2026, as reported by TheEnergyMag. This figure surpasses the 20,000 BTC sold in Q2 2022 during the crypto bear market triggered by the Terra-Luna ecosystem collapse, setting a new record for BTC miner sales in a single quarter, according to TheMinerMag.

The increased sales occur as hashprice, a critical metric for miner profitability that represents computing cost, remains at record low levels under $35 per petahash/second per day (PH/s), based on data from Hashrate Index. This $35 PH/s level is the breakeven point for many Bitcoin miners, especially those operating older mining machines. The current hashprice of approximately $33 PH/s per day places about 20% of the mining industry in unprofitable territory. Hashprice for BTC miners has been declining since July 2025 and now sits at about $33 PH/s per day, according to Hashrate Index.

The mining industry faces increased competition due to a rising hashrate, reduced block rewards, and macroeconomic challenges. The Bitcoin Miner Reserve, which tracks all BTC held by miners, has been gradually decreasing since 2023, as noted by CryptoQuant. Bitcoin miners collectively held over 1.86 million BTC at the end of 2023 but currently hold about 1.8 million BTC. Miners periodically sell portions of their BTC to cover operating expenses, but lower crypto prices and rising energy costs have forced some to offload coins they would have held in their corporate treasuries. Asset manager CoinShares anticipates further capitulation among higher-cost operators in the first half of 2026 unless BTC's price recovers significantly.

In contrast to the miners' selling, Bitcoin treasury companies like Strategy continue to acquire BTC. Michael Saylor, co-founder of the largest Bitcoin treasury company, indicated earlier this week that Strategy is purchasing more BTC as the price retreats from a local high of over $73,000 reached this week. "Think bigger," Saylor remarked on Sunday, sharing Strategy's BTC purchase history chart, which has become synonymous with imminent BTC acquisitions.

data.title: Public Crypto Miners Exceed 2025 BTC Sales in Q1 2026
data.description: Public crypto miners have sold over 32,000 BTC in the first quarter of 2026, surpassing their total sales for the entire year of 2025. According to NS3.AI, data from Hashrate Index indicates that the hashprice stands at approximately $33 per PH/s per day, which is below the $35 breakeven point for many miners. Additionally, CryptoQuant data reveals that the amount of BTC held by miners has decreased from over 1.86 million at the end of 2023 to about 1.8 million at the time of publication.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences Research
data.description: OpenAI has introduced a new model named GPT-Rosalind, designed to support research in the life sciences sector. According to Jin10, the model will initially be available as a research preview to select enterprise clients.

data.title: ECB Official Cautions Against Premature Rate Path Signals
data.description: On April 17, ECB Governing Council member Nagel stated that releasing clear signals about future interest rate paths before this month's rate decision would be a mistake. According to Jin10, he mentioned that policymakers currently lack sufficient information to determine whether the surge in energy costs will keep inflation high in the long term, which will dictate whether a policy response is necessary. "We need to maintain flexibility, and it is inappropriate to assert that rates will move in a certain direction now. I am not ready to make a commitment in advance," Nagel said. Insiders revealed that the ECB is inclined to keep rates unchanged at this meeting, but in the rapidly changing environment, a rate hike is not ruled out. Nagel noted that Germany's economy performed reasonably well at the beginning of the year, but momentum weakened due to the impact of the Iran war, with economic growth expected to be 0.3 percentage points lower than previously forecast this year. He also warned that investor sentiment is overly optimistic. "The current market is relatively optimistic because of the widespread assumption that lasting peace will be achieved in the Middle East and energy prices will fall," he said. "The central bank's duty is to ensure that if this scenario does not materialize, it will not have spillover effects on financial stability."

data.title: Lido DAO Approves $20 Million Buyback as LDO Surges 30% in 30 Days
data.description: Lido DAO has sanctioned a $20 million buyback initiative, resulting in a 30% increase in LDO's value over the past month, even as other major DeFi tokens experienced declines. According to NS3.AI, the approved plan allows the DAO to utilize up to 10,000 stETH, approximately valued at $23 million, for the repurchase of LDO tokens. CoinGecko data indicates that LDO was trading at $0.42, marking a 12% rise in the last 24 hours.
